user.title: HA Exporter
user.description: Send Home Assistant statistics and states to a remote REST API.

data_description.endpoint: Base URL of your receiving API. The integration appends /ingest automatically. Pushes, retries, and the offline buffer use this URL first (use the server that must be reliable, e.g. production).
data_description.second_endpoint: Optional second base URL. The same bearer token and payload are sent to both URLs on every successful push (e.g. mirror to a dev server). If the second URL is down, uploads to the primary still succeed (warning in the log). Only the primary controls retries and backoff.
data_description.interval: How often buffered data is flushed to the remote API. Minimum 30 seconds.
data_description.include_energy: If enabled, long-term statistics for every entity configured in the Energy dashboard are pushed hourly.
data_description.extra_entities: State changes for these entities are pushed in real time (batched).
data_description.initial_backfill_days: When the integration starts for the first time (or after a remote reset), how many days of long-term statistics to upload from Home Assistant. 0 disables auto-hydrate.
data_description.electricity_price_entity: Normally the Energy dashboard price (entity or fixed number on each grid consumer) is used automatically. Set this only to force a different sensor for €/kWh when estimating grid, PV, and battery amounts.
data_description.gas_price_entity: Normally the Energy dashboard gas price (entity or fixed number on the gas source) is used automatically. Set this only to force a different sensor for €/m³ when estimating gas cost.
data_description.battery_available_kwh_entity: If set, Summary shows this under live battery power: either **remaining energy** (kWh / Wh / …) or a **state-of-charge percentage** sensor (unit % or a value like 34%). For kWh-only sensors, optionally set usable capacity below to draw the 0–100% ring; with a % sensor the capacity field is not needed.
data_description.battery_capacity_kwh_entity: Usable energy when 100% full (same unit as remaining). With “remaining” set, the energy distribution diagram draws a segmented charge ring (0–100%).

error.invalid_url: The endpoint must be a valid http(s) URL.
error.same_as_primary: The second URL must differ from the primary endpoint.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one HA Exporter instance can be configured.

data_description.endpoint: Base URL the integration POSTs to (append /ingest). Primary: retries and buffer ack use this URL.
data_description.second_endpoint: Optional mirror. Same token and full payload on each successful push. Failures are logged only.

push_now.name: Push now
push_now.description: Flush the buffer immediately to the remote API.

backfill.name: Backfill statistics
backfill.description: Re-export long-term statistics for every configured Energy dashboard entity.

days.name: Days
days.description: How many days of history to re-export. Takes precedence over Hours.

hours.name: Hours
hours.description: How many hours of history to re-export. Ignored if Days is given.

clear_cursors.name: Clear cursors
clear_cursors.description: Forget the per-statistic 'last exported' bookmark before collecting, so everything is re-sent.

reset_remote.name: Reset remote data
reset_remote.description: Wipe stored time-series on the remote API for this instance, then re-hydrate it.

days.name: Days to re-hydrate
days.description: How many days of statistics to re-push after the wipe. 0 means wipe only.

full.name: Full wipe
full.description: Also delete the stored energy dashboard configuration on the server.
